posts.title: Video Title — 1
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version": "0.3.1", "atoms": [], "cards": [["html", {"html": "

Somewhere between the brief and the deadline lives the actual work. This is generic demo content so you can judge the layout honestly.

The setup

Every good system starts with a constraint: frame the color 1:1, crop out the noise.

  • Start with the audience, not the tool
  • Measure twice, publish once
  • Delete more than you add

The details

const frame = (life) => life.filter(m => m.signal > m.noise);
Life looks better when you frame the color 1:1.

Wrap up

If this reads well in all six theme modes, ship it.

posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

This one exists to test chapters. The table below becomes a clickable chapter rail; the timestamps written inline in this paragraph become tappable too — jump to the demo at 4:05 or the recap at 9:20.

Chapters

TimeSection
0:00Intro
1:30The setup
4:05Building it
9:20Wrap up

Notes

Everything here is generic demo content so you can judge the layout honestly.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: A video with a real timestamp table and inline timestamps — tap any of them, on desktop or mobile.

posts.title: Video Title — No Thumbnail
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

This post has no feature image. The video card should fall back to the YouTube thumbnail and, on hover, autoplay a muted preview pulled from this embed.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: No feature image on purpose — the card should pull the YouTube thumbnail and hover-preview the reel.

posts.title: Short — Vertical No Image
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

A vertical short. It lives in the Shorts & Reels rail on /videos, rendered in 9:16.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}

posts.title: Kickoff — the idea & constraints
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

Day zero. Wrote down the one-line pitch, the hard constraints, and made the first commit. Nothing works yet — that is the point.

Scope

Kept it deliberately small so it could actually ship.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}

posts.title: Wireframes & the data model
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

Sketched the screens on paper, then modelled the data. Two rewrites of the schema before it felt right.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}

posts.title: Building the core loop
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

The fun part: made the main flow work end to end. Cut two features to protect the deadline.

Trade-offs

Chose boring, reliable tech over clever.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}

posts.title: Ship it
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

Shipped. Wrote the README, recorded a demo, and pushed it live. 18 days start to ship.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}

posts.title: Refactor this function
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"
You are a senior engineer. Refactor the function I paste below for readability, without changing its behaviour. Then list the 3 most important changes as bullets.

Paste your function right after the prompt. You get the refactor plus a short rationale — perfect for a PR description.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: A reliable refactor prompt that keeps behaviour and explains the changes.

posts.title: Explain it like I ship tomorrow
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"
Explain [TOPIC] to a working developer who ships tomorrow. Skip history and theory. Give me: the one-line mental model, the 3 things that bite people, and a minimal example.

Great for getting productive with a new tool in five minutes instead of an afternoon.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Cuts the theory — gives you the practical version fast.

posts.title: Debounce in vanilla JS
posts.custom_excerpt: The trailing debounce I paste into every project.

posts.title: Full-bleed CSS utility
posts.custom_excerpt: Break an element out of a centered container to the viewport edges.

posts.title: Spoke at my first conference
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

My hands were shaking through the first slide. By the demo I forgot to be nervous. The best part was the hallway conversations afterwards.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Terrified backstage, buzzing after. Would do it again tomorrow.

posts.title: Lived a month in Tokyo
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

Worked mornings, wandered afternoons. The city rewired how I think about small, careful design.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: A tiny apartment, a train pass, and a notebook that filled up fast.

posts.title: Shipped my first real app
posts.mobiledoc:
{"version":"0.3.1","atoms":[],"cards":[["html",{"html":"

It was held together with tape and hope, but it was live, and people used it. That first non-family signup email is still saved.

"}]],"markups":[],"sections":[[10,0],[1,"p",[]]]}
posts.custom_excerpt: Ten users, one of them not related to me. Felt like a million.

posts.title: See the northern lights
posts.mobiledoc:
